title: Next-Lap Hazard Model Instead Of Plain Binary Only
summary: This is a time-to-event problem with censoring at race end or tyre changes, which matches survival modeling more naturally than independent row classification.
rationale: This is a time-to-event problem with censoring at race end or tyre changes, which matches survival modeling more naturally than independent row classification. Time-varying Cox and discrete hazard models are built exactly for evolving covariates and one-step event risk.
implementation_hint: Reshape each driver-race trajectory into start/stop intervals ending in pit event or censoring. Train a small sidecar with CoxTimeVaryingFitter or a discrete-time LogisticHazard model stratified by wet/dry or slick/wet regime, then use the predicted hazard at the current interval as a blend feature or direct ensemble member.
expected_effect: Can regularize sequential consistency and improve calibration of pit probability curves across a stint, especially around rising hazard zones.
risk: Survival sidecars can underperform if the long-format construction leaks future state or if censored race-end intervals overwhelm true pit events.
